hypothesis: Introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 and TM5, while reducing off-target binding to SSTR3/SSTR5. This will increase the selectivity margin by improving SSTR2 complementarity and reducing off-target affinity.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: strong SSTR2 binding is achievable, but selectivity over SSTR3/SSTR5 is the limiting factor. The next iteration should focus on disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions.

proposed_changes.rationale: To improve selectivity, focus on non-pharmacophore positions that are likely to form conserved-pocket contacts with off-target SSTR3/SSTR5. Mutations at these positions can disrupt off-target binding while preserving key pharmacophore residues.
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved-pocket interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while preserving the pharmacophore (FWKT at positions 7-10) and cysteine residues (Cys3/Cys14).
